
---
title: "Chroma SaaS – System Design Documentation"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: false
---

# Project Overview

This document describes the architecture for a **Chroma Color Mixing and Inventory SaaS platform**.

The goal of the system is to help hair salons:

- Track **color formulas used on customers**
- Track **inventory of color products**
- Calculate **cost per service**
- Maintain **customer history**
- Support **multiple salons (multi-tenant SaaS)**

The system allows a salon employee to:

1. Log in
2. Select a customer
3. Mix color formulas
4. Save the formula
5. Automatically deduct inventory
6. Track cost of color usage

The design is built so the platform can support **many different salons using the same system**.

---

# User Stories

## Salon Owner

As a **salon owner**, I want to:

- Manage my **salon locations**
- Add and manage **employees**
- Track **inventory levels**
- Track **product usage costs**
- See which products need **restocking**
- Maintain a **catalog of products we use**

## Stylist / Employee

As a **stylist**, I want to:

- Log into the system
- Select a customer
- Record the **formula used for their hair color**
- See the **customer's past formulas**
- Automatically update inventory when color is used

## Salon Manager

As a **manager**, I want to:

- Monitor inventory levels
- Track product usage
- Identify waste
- Track service costs

## SaaS Platform Owner

As the **software provider**, I want:

- Multiple salons using the same platform
- Data separation between salons
- Ability to scale to thousands of salons

---

# Industry Workflow

Typical workflow inside a professional salon:

1. Stylist places bowl on digital scale
2. Adds color products
3. Records grams used
4. Applies mixture
5. Saves formula for future visits

Example:

```text
Customer: Sarah

30g 7N
15g 7A
45ml developer
```

The system records the formula and deducts inventory.

---

# Inventory Tracking Method

Inventory is tracked in **grams or milliliters** rather than tubes.

Example:

```text
Product: Redken Shades EQ 09N
Tube size: 60g
Inventory: 4 tubes

Total available = 240g
```

When used:

```text
Used: 30g
Remaining: 210g
```

This allows precise cost calculation.

---

# Why Tracking in Grams / ML Is Better

The system should track inventory in **grams or milliliters**, not just by counting tubes or bottles.

Why this is better:

- Stylists use **partial amounts** from multiple products
- The app can calculate the **real cost of a service**
- Inventory becomes more accurate
- Waste can be tracked better
- The system works across many brands and sizes

Example:

```text
20g 7N
10g 7A
30ml developer
```

If the system only tracks whole tubes, it cannot measure this formula correctly.

---

# System Architecture

The system is designed with a **multi-tenant SaaS structure**.

Key components:

- Tenant (Salon Business)
- Locations (Branches)
- Users (Employees)
- Customers
- Product Catalog
- Inventory
- Formulas

---

# Entity Relationship Diagram

This is the **simple relationship view** of the system.

```{mermaid}
erDiagram

TENANTS ||--o{ LOCATIONS : has
TENANTS ||--o{ USERS : employs
TENANTS ||--o{ CUSTOMERS : owns

BRANDS ||--o{ PRODUCT_LINES : contains
PRODUCT_LINES ||--o{ PRODUCTS : contains

TENANTS ||--o{ TENANT_PRODUCTS : configures
PRODUCTS ||--o{ TENANT_PRODUCTS : mapped_to

LOCATIONS ||--o{ INVENTORY_ITEMS : stores
TENANT_PRODUCTS ||--o{ INVENTORY_ITEMS : tracked_as

CUSTOMERS ||--o{ FORMULAS : receives
USERS ||--o{ FORMULAS : creates
LOCATIONS ||--o{ FORMULAS : occurs_at

FORMULAS ||--o{ FORMULA_ITEMS : contains
TENANT_PRODUCTS ||--o{ FORMULA_ITEMS : used_in

INVENTORY_ITEMS ||--o{ INVENTORY_TXNS : logs
FORMULAS ||--o{ INVENTORY_TXNS : triggers
USERS ||--o{ INVENTORY_TXNS : performed_by
```

---

# Detailed ER Diagram With Fields

This is the **detailed field-level ER diagram** you can use when drawing the system in Draw.io.

```{mermaid}
erDiagram
  TENANTS ||--o{ LOCATIONS : has
  TENANTS ||--o{ USERS : employs
  TENANTS ||--o{ CUSTOMERS : owns
  BRANDS ||--o{ PRODUCT_LINES : has
  PRODUCT_LINES ||--o{ PRODUCTS : includes
  TENANTS ||--o{ TENANT_PRODUCTS : configures
  PRODUCTS ||--o{ TENANT_PRODUCTS : mapped_to
  LOCATIONS ||--o{ INVENTORY_ITEMS : stores
  TENANT_PRODUCTS ||--o{ INVENTORY_ITEMS : tracked_as
  CUSTOMERS ||--o{ FORMULAS : has
  USERS ||--o{ FORMULAS : creates
  LOCATIONS ||--o{ FORMULAS : occurs_at
  FORMULAS ||--o{ FORMULA_ITEMS : contains
  TENANT_PRODUCTS ||--o{ FORMULA_ITEMS : used_in
  INVENTORY_ITEMS ||--o{ INVENTORY_TXNS : logs
  FORMULAS ||--o{ INVENTORY_TXNS : triggers
  USERS ||--o{ INVENTORY_TXNS : performed_by

  TENANTS {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string plan
    string status
    datetime created_at
  }

  LOCATIONS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid tenant_id FK
    string name
    string address
    string timezone
    datetime created_at
  }

  USERS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid tenant_id FK
    uuid default_location_id FK
    string full_name
    string email
    string password_hash
    string role
    boolean is_active
    datetime last_login_at
    datetime created_at
  }

  CUSTOMERS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid tenant_id FK
    uuid home_location_id FK
    string full_name
    string phone
    string email
    string notes
    datetime created_at
  }

  BRANDS {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string website_url
    datetime created_at
  }

  PRODUCT_LINES {
    uuid id PK
    uuid brand_id FK
    string name
    string category
    datetime created_at
  }

  PRODUCTS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid product_line_id FK
    string sku
    string code
    string name
    string tone_family
    float pack_size_value
    string pack_size_unit
    boolean is_active
    datetime created_at
  }

  TENANT_PRODUCTS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid tenant_id FK
    uuid product_id FK
    string custom_name
    boolean is_enabled
    string tracking_unit
    float default_unit_cost
    string currency
    datetime created_at
  }

  INVENTORY_ITEMS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid tenant_id FK
    uuid location_id FK
    uuid tenant_product_id FK
    float on_hand_qty
    float reorder_level_qty
    datetime updated_at
    datetime created_at
  }

  INVENTORY_TXNS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid tenant_id FK
    uuid inventory_item_id FK
    uuid formula_id FK
    uuid user_id FK
    string txn_type
    float qty_delta
    float unit_cost_at_time
    string note
    datetime created_at
  }

  FORMULAS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid tenant_id FK
    uuid location_id FK
    uuid customer_id FK
    uuid created_by_user_id FK
    string formula_name
    string service_type
    string notes
    datetime created_at
  }

  FORMULA_ITEMS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid formula_id FK
    uuid tenant_product_id FK
    float amount_used
    float cost_at_time
    datetime created_at
  }
```

---

# Why the System Uses a Tenant Product Catalog

The system includes a **tenant-specific product catalog** using the `tenant_products` table.

This design allows each salon to manage its own list of products while still using a shared global product database.

In practice, different salons:

- Use different brands and product lines
- Purchase products from different distributors
- Pay different prices for the same product
- Sometimes rename or organize products differently inside their salon

Because of this, the system separates the **global product catalog** from the **salon-specific catalog**.

## System Structure

```text
Global Product Catalog
Brands → Product Lines → Products
            │
            ▼
Tenant Product Catalog
Tenant Products
            │
            ▼
Salon Operations
Inventory / Formulas
```

### Explanation

**Global Catalog**

The global catalog stores product information shared across the platform:

- `brands`
- `product_lines`
- `products`

This includes manufacturer information such as product names, shade codes, and packaging sizes.

**Tenant Catalog**

Each salon has its own catalog layer using:

- `tenant_products`

This allows each salon to:

- Enable only the products they use
- Store their own cost information
- Customize product naming if needed
- Configure how inventory is tracked

**Salon Operations**

The operational parts of the system use the tenant-specific catalog:

- `inventory_items`
- `formulas`
- `formula_items`

These tables reference `tenant_products` so that inventory tracking and cost calculations reflect the **specific configuration of each salon**.

This structure keeps the global product catalog clean while allowing every salon to manage its own product setup.

---

# Table Descriptions

## 1) tenants

**Purpose:** One paying salon business (SaaS customer).

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `name` (VARCHAR(150))
- `plan` (VARCHAR(50), NULL)
- `status` (VARCHAR(30)) — active, trial, suspended
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- Owns locations, users, customers, tenant-specific catalog, inventory, formulas.
- This table is the main boundary for multi-tenant data separation.

---

## 2) locations

**Purpose:** Tenant can have multiple branches.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `tenant_id` (UUID, FK → tenants.id)
- `name` (VARCHAR(150))
- `address` (TEXT, NULL)
- `timezone` (VARCHAR(50), NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `tenants 1—* locations`
- One salon business can operate from many physical branches.
- Inventory, customers, and formulas are often tied to a location.

---

## 3) users

**Purpose:** Staff login accounts.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `tenant_id` (UUID, FK → tenants.id)
- `default_location_id` (UUID, FK → locations.id, NULL)
- `full_name` (VARCHAR(150))
- `email` (VARCHAR(255))
- `password_hash` (TEXT)
- `role` (VARCHAR(20)) — ADMIN | MANAGER | EMPLOYEE
- `is_active` (BOOLEAN)
- `last_login_at` (TIMESTAMP, NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `tenants 1—* users`
- `locations 1—* users` through `default_location_id` for default branch assignment
- Users create formulas and perform inventory transactions.
- This table is required for authentication, audit trail, and role-based permissions.

---

## 4) customers

**Purpose:** Salon clients and their service history.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `tenant_id` (UUID, FK → tenants.id)
- `home_location_id` (UUID, FK → locations.id)
- `full_name` (VARCHAR(150))
- `phone` (VARCHAR(30), NULL)
- `email` (VARCHAR(255), NULL)
- `notes` (TEXT, NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `tenants 1—* customers`
- `locations 1—* customers`
- `customers 1—* formulas`
- A customer belongs to a salon business and usually has a home branch.
- Their formula history is linked through the `formulas` table.

---

# Product Catalog

## 5) brands

**Purpose:** Manufacturer of products such as Redken or Wella.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `name` (VARCHAR(150))
- `website_url` (TEXT, NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `brands 1—* product_lines`
- A brand groups many product families under one manufacturer.

---

## 6) product_lines

**Purpose:** Product family under a brand, such as Shades EQ.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `brand_id` (UUID, FK → brands.id)
- `name` (VARCHAR(150))
- `category` (VARCHAR(30)) — COLOR | DEVELOPER | TONER | TREATMENT
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `brands 1—* product_lines`
- `product_lines 1—* products`
- This helps group products into meaningful families for filtering and administration.

---

## 7) products

**Purpose:** Global product definitions shared across the platform.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `product_line_id` (UUID, FK → product_lines.id)
- `sku` (VARCHAR(80), NULL)
- `code` (VARCHAR(30)) — example: 09N
- `name` (VARCHAR(150))
- `tone_family` (VARCHAR(50), NULL) - optional (N, G, V, etc.)
- `pack_size_value` (DECIMAL(10,2), NULL)
- `pack_size_unit` (VARCHAR(10), NULL) — G | ML
- `is_active` (BOOLEAN)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `product_lines 1—* products`
- `products 1—* tenant_products`
- This is the master catalog. It should not hold salon-specific pricing or usage rules.

---

# Tenant Product Catalog

## 8) tenant_products

**Purpose:** What a specific tenant actually carries and how that tenant tracks cost.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `tenant_id` (UUID, FK → tenants.id)
- `product_id` (UUID, FK → products.id)
- `custom_name` (VARCHAR(150), NULL)
- `is_enabled` (BOOLEAN)
- `tracking_unit` (VARCHAR(10)) — G | ML
- `default_unit_cost` (DECIMAL(12,4), NULL)
- `currency` (VARCHAR(10), NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `tenants 1—* tenant_products`
- `products 1—* tenant_products`
- This table allows each salon to choose products, rename them if needed, and store its own cost rules.
- It is the most important table for SaaS scalability.

---

# Inventory Management

## 9) inventory_items

**Purpose:** Current stock per location per tenant product.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `tenant_id` (UUID, FK → tenants.id)
- `location_id` (UUID, FK → locations.id)
- `tenant_product_id` (UUID, FK → tenant_products.id)
- `on_hand_qty` (DECIMAL(12,2))
- `reorder_level_qty` (DECIMAL(12,2), NULL)
- `updated_at` (TIMESTAMP)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `locations 1—* inventory_items`
- `tenant_products 1—* inventory_items`
- Each branch can have different stock for the same product.
- The quantity is stored in grams or milliliters for precise tracking.

**Suggested constraint:**

- Unique (`location_id`, `tenant_product_id`) so the same product is not duplicated in one location.

---

## 10) inventory_txns

**Purpose:** Full audit log of every stock movement.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `tenant_id` (UUID, FK → tenants.id)
- `inventory_item_id` (UUID, FK → inventory_items.id)
- `formula_id` (UUID, FK → formulas.id, NULL)
- `user_id` (UUID, FK → users.id)
- `txn_type` (VARCHAR(20)) — PURCHASE | CONSUME | ADJUST | WASTE | RETURN
- `qty_delta` (DECIMAL(12,2))
- `unit_cost_at_time` (DECIMAL(12,4), NULL)
- `note` (TEXT, NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `inventory_items 1—* inventory_txns`
- `users 1—* inventory_txns`
- `formulas 1—* inventory_txns`
- Every stock increase or decrease should be recorded here.
- This table supports cost history, auditability, and reporting.

---

# Formula Tracking

## 11) formulas

**Purpose:** A saved color mix recipe for a customer visit.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `tenant_id` (UUID, FK → tenants.id)
- `location_id` (UUID, FK → locations.id)
- `customer_id` (UUID, FK → customers.id)
- `created_by_user_id` (UUID, FK → users.id)
- `formula_name` (VARCHAR(150), NULL)
- `service_type` (VARCHAR(50), NULL)
- `notes` (TEXT, NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `customers 1—* formulas`
- `users 1—* formulas`
- `locations 1—* formulas`
- A formula must belong to one customer, one branch, and one employee.
- This table is the center of customer color history.

---

## 12) formula_items

**Purpose:** Products used inside a formula and the amount used for each one.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `formula_id` (UUID, FK → formulas.id)
- `tenant_product_id` (UUID, FK → tenant_products.id)
- `amount_used` (DECIMAL(12,2))
- `cost_at_time` (DECIMAL(12,4), NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `formulas 1—* formula_items`
- `tenant_products 1—* formula_items`
- A single formula can contain many color products and developer products.
- This table makes it possible to calculate total service cost and deduct inventory correctly.

---

# Relationship Summary

- `tenants 1—* locations`
- `tenants 1—* users`
- `tenants 1—* customers`
- `brands 1—* product_lines`
- `product_lines 1—* products`
- `tenants 1—* tenant_products`
- `products 1—* tenant_products`
- `locations 1—* inventory_items`
- `tenant_products 1—* inventory_items`
- `inventory_items 1—* inventory_txns`
- `users 1—* inventory_txns`
- `customers 1—* formulas`
- `users 1—* formulas`
- `locations 1—* formulas`
- `formulas 1—* formula_items`
- `tenant_products 1—* formula_items`
- `formulas 1—* inventory_txns`

---

# Example Formula Record

Example stored formula:

```text
Customer: Sarah
Stylist: Mike

20g 7N
10g 7A
30ml developer
```

The system:

1. Saves the formula
2. Creates formula items
3. Creates inventory transactions
4. Updates inventory levels
5. Preserves customer history for the next visit

---

# End of Document
